authorization.note: Reproducible plan only. NO pod is created until a sealed budget authorizes the lane.

auto_terminate_triggers.budget: cumulative spend reaches max_total_spend OR pod price exceeds max_hourly_price -> terminate
auto_terminate_triggers.completion: science program completes and artifacts+receipts pulled back -> terminate
auto_terminate_triggers.drain: operator/controller drain signal or lost controller contact (on_disconnect) -> terminate
auto_terminate_triggers.failure: unrecoverable job failure or repeated crash-loop -> terminate
auto_terminate_triggers.integrity: source/artifact hash mismatch, driver/compute-capability out of contract, or image digest drift -> terminate

container_digest_plan.base_image_family: nvidia/cuda runtime (devel variant for kernel build)

build_provenance.note: Digest is populated by the reproducible build step at authorization time; unset while BLOCKED.

container_digest_plan.digest_pinning: image referenced as <registry>/<repo>@sha256:<digest>; tags are advisory only
container_digest_plan.strategy: immutable, digest-pinned image; build reproducibly then reference by sha256 digest, never a floating tag

cuda_version.note: Target chosen for Ampere/Hopper support; pin exact toolkit in the digest-pinned image.

driver_reqs.note: Driver is host-provided by RunPod; validate at pod boot before any work runs.

library_versions.note: Exact patch versions are frozen in the image lockfile at build; must match the Apple-lane science deps where they intersect.

note: Reproducible RunPod/container plan for the CUDA backend. PLAN ONLY: no pod created, no spend incurred. Provisioning is gated on a sealed, operator-signed budget (Part 28). One science, two backends: CUDA contributes device operators only; identity/quality/Gravity/evidence are shared.

one_science_two_backends.parity_requirement: CUDA outputs must reproduce the Apple-lane artifact identity and pass the identical quality contract; divergence beyond sealed tolerance FAILS parity and the lane is not promoted.
one_science_two_backends.principle: One science, two backends. The Apple/MPS lane and the CUDA/cloud lane run the SAME science: same source artifact, same identity/provenance, same quality contract, same Gravity search, same evidence ledger and sealing. Only backend-specific OPERATORS differ (device kernels, memory placement, dtype packing). Nothing science-bearing is backend-specific.
shared_across_backends: evidence ledger, ByteLedger accounting, and sha256 sealing

runpod_adapter.description: Backend-specific provisioning/teardown operator. Every op is a no-op (refused) while the lane is BLOCKED.

cost_monitor.op: track cumulative spend vs max_total_spend + per-hour vs max_hourly_price; arm auto-terminate

logs.op: stream pod logs to the controller for evidence

network_volume.op: create/attach persistent network volume for source + artifacts + receipts

pod_create.op: create pod from template on an approved_gpu_class within max_hourly_price

status.op: poll pod lifecycle + GPU health + compute-capability/driver validation

template.op: register/pin the pod template (digest-pinned image, env, volume mount, ports)

terminate.op: tear down pod + dispose/retain volume per policy; emit sealed teardown receipt

teardown.note: No pod may leak: terminate is idempotent and emits a sealed receipt for every lifecycle end.
